data.title: Watsco (NYSE: WSO) to hold Q2 earnings call on July 30, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. (EDT). Senior management will share results.
data.description: Miami-based company Watsco, Inc. (NYSE: WSO) revealed plans to conduct its Q2 2025 conference call on July 30, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. (EDT). The call will include prepared remarks on the quarterly results, as well as a Q&A session with the senior management team.

data.title: B. Braun introduces 7 new heparin sodium injection products, including 25,000 Units in 0.45% solution.
data.description: In Bethlehem, Pennsylvania on July 18, 2025, B. Braun Medical Inc. introduced its latest product, Heparin Sodium Injections, to the American market. This new addition to their portfolio contains 25,000 Units of Heparin in 0.45% solution and further solidifies their position as a top provider in smart infusion therapy and pain management.

data.title: Low Cost Pet Vax in Texas adjusts outdoor clinic hours due to summer heat, now opening at 8AM on Saturdays and 9AM on Sundays.
data.description: In Irving, Texas, on July 18, 2025, Low Cost Pet Vax has made changes to its summer schedule due to rising temperatures in the state. From mid July onwards, the outdoor pet vaccination pop ups will now open at 8:00 AM instead of the previous time of 9:00 AM. This adjustment aims to provide a cooler environment for pets and their owners as the "dog days" of summer bring high heat levels.

data.title: Sports and lottery websites headed to Toronto for 2025 street battle finale, drivers Callum Ilott and Louis Foster.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global internet penetration rate has reached a new milestone of 60 percent, with over 4.7 billion people now having internet access. Additionally, the number of social media users has exceeded 4.3 billion, indicating a significant increase in online communication and connectivity. The report also highlights that mobile phone usage continues to rise, with more than 5.1 billion unique mobile users worldwide. These numbers demonstrate a growing reliance on digital technology for communication and access to information on a global scale.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller supports July rate cut, ready to replace Powell if Trump chooses. He backs job shift by 2026, citing need based on labor market data.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller strongly advocated for a rate cut in July and expressed readiness to potentially take over as Federal Reserve Chair if President Trump offers him the position. In a recent interview on Bloomberg TV, Waller, along with fellow policymaker Michelle Bowman, voiced support for a rate cut in the upcoming July meeting, aligning with the President's call for looser monetary policy. Waller not only endorsed a rate reduction but also stated his willingness to assume the role of Federal Reserve Chair in 2026 if nominated. This comes amidst mounting speculation about Jerome Powell's future. Waller emphasized the urgency for a rate cut due to concerning labor market indicators, despite seemingly stable headline figures. His stance highlights a shift towards a more accommodative monetary policy amid economic uncertainties. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
